notes: Fibre Channel SAN dominant in enterprise; HP led open SAN with 31.2% share Q3 2003; grew 15.7% YoY; still widely deployed
File-based storage networking; EMC led NAS market 2003; NAS persisted and evolved to unified/object storage
IP-based block storage; standardized 2003; gained traction as cost-effective SAN alternative; widely deployed in mid-market
Backup and archive tape libraries; StorageTek was leader; tape automation remained viable through 2020s for archive
External controller-based disk storage; $12.89B market 2003 per Gartner; grew 6% in 2003; shifted to SSD and NVMe by 2020s
Resource pooling and abstraction layer; Hill predicted this would transform data centers; became foundational (VMware VSAN etc)
Policy-driven provisioning and management; Hill's central long-term prediction; materialized as software-defined storage
Concept of storage as on-demand utility service; Hill's prescient framing anticipated cloud storage (AWS S3 launched 2006)
Single-image namespace across distributed storage; materialized as object storage (S3) and distributed file systems (HDFS)
